source_note: Global spec-grade trailing-12M default rate was 3.2% at end-2019 per Moody's (cited via S&P Global MI). US-specific year-end 2019 figure not extracted from free summaries.
Global spec-grade trailing-12M rate ended 2020 at 6.7% (S&P Global MI citing Moody's). US-specific figure not cleanly extracted; Moody's forecast peak ~9.1% by Mar 2021.
Moody's forecast US spec-grade trailing-12M peak of ~9.1% in Mar 2021 (S&P Global MI summary).
Moody's-rated defaults fell to very low levels by end-2021; exact US spec-grade TTM year-end figure not in free summaries.
Moody's: 12-month trailing default rate for US non-financial spec-grade companies was 1.5% at end-Sep 2022 (Moody's Q1 2024 Credit Outlook, via cfodive.com).
Global spec-grade TTM rose to 2.9% at end-2022 (Moody's via LinkedIn). US-specific year-end 2022 rate not cleanly extracted.
US spec-grade TTM default rate closed 2023 at 5.6% (Moody's December 2023 Default Report).
US spec-grade 12-month realized default rate was 5.6% as of Mar 2024 (Moody's).
US spec-grade TTM year-end 2024 not cleanly extracted from free summaries; distressed exchanges = highest annual share on record.
Moody's: through Sep 2025, US issuer-weighted trailing 12M HY bond default rate = 3.7%; leveraged loan default rate = 5.9%. Bond figure used here as the spec-grade bond rate.
Moody's projected US HY realized default rate = 3.2% for CY2025; loan rate projected 7.5%. Projection figure — confirm with actual year-end report.
Moody's projects US HY default rate rising above 4% by Q1 2026; leveraged loan rate 7.9% in Q1 2026.
